C[Mef:  hmm,  not going to lie, p16  and o17  we kinda autopilot moves,  but if black cuts here it might get complicated
Mef:  after the cut n17 looks ok though I guess.  The counter cut might work also but that is much less straightforward, I would have to study that.
Mef: n17 is more simple though,  makes q17 and n16 miai
C[Mef: Now O14 - Q15 are basically forced? then it gets interesting.....
C[Mef:  This could be tricky,  there is a lot of aji in the corner,  but I don't know for sure black needs to defend right away
C[Mef: I can play it slow, and just leave the aji
C[Mef: I feel like this lets me settle a little too easily,  the center might become a target now
C[Mef: a normal shape for an exchange  like this would be cutting but I don't want to both strengthen his left side and make it easy to bail out the top stones
C[Mef: I've been playing on my phone,  and some of my last moves were hasty... I'm lucky black didn't take advantage.   This last move leaves a lot of aji in his own position.
Mef: c16  would fix his shape and give his stones more liberties
C[Mef:  Everything is relatively calm... let's go after his potentially weak group
C[Mef:  If I can keep black running I can  solidify the top, try to get a foothold for jumping into the left,  and make the center dame
C[Mef: I've been playing via phone for a while so I haven't been able to comment at all
Mef: I chased black a bit into the middle, and jumped in with C4 (though perhaps B4/B5 would have been better)
Mef: The left side got solid, but I was able to live in sente, so I can't complain too much
Mef: At that point it feels like it might turn into an endgame battle, which I'm not terribly strong at, but need to work on so that's fine
Mef: The big points I see as of Move 99 in no particular order - C16, S8, J4, H14, Q7, maybe F6, F10?
Mef: I take j4 because this neutralizes M3, and feels pretty big for white.  Also F5 could be an interesting follow up later
Mef: P7 is pretty big, but I'm not sure black can really follow it up strongly with the Aji at Q7.  if Black plays O7 as a follow up I'm happy with N6.
Mef: If fighting breaks out though, my right group might get cut off, so I will take a big point and support it.
Mef: F10 is to prevent the left from getting too big...also it holds out a small chance that I might get to chase J9 a little bit more (maybe into F5?)
Mef: N7 - I think this is an overplay, though it might turn into a fight
Mef: Q6 offers a trade...black can cut (P9) or connect (R8) but white would eat into the corner.
Mef: R6 black refuses to give up the corner - this could be crazy
Mef: fighting ensues, bringing us back to current game
Mef: I think black made a mistake earlier, and should have exchanged Q8 for R8.  At this point white doesn't have to R8 because N6 would be a success
Mef: if move 127 is Q8, white has to block otherwise the P3 stones are easily killed....but even after that it looks hard for them to live
C[Mef: Now we see if black can find a second eye
Mef: If B can't, right now the middle group of his has about 6-10 liberties depending on how the fight for the second eye plays out...
C[Mef: We have moved into endgame...
C[Mef: I count ~65 for white, maybe 55 for black?  I should double check this later
Mef: I'm trying to sneak in plays that are fairly large and possibly sente for both sides.  I really didn't want B to get S10, and I was curious to see how B would respond to Q1
Mef: if I come away with sente I'll be looking at either K9 or O18
C[Mef: This could become a very heavy ko for black
C[Mef: counting again, W has 60 + komi + 4 extra captures, call it 70, B has 50-ish?
